description_long: This guard stone is a robust, monolithic object shaped in a stylized, upright human form with arms crossed over the chest. The surface is uniformly matte and appears to be made from a dense, stone-like material, giving it a solid and imposing presence. The facial features are minimal, with only a slight indentation for the eyes and mouth, and the body is blocky with simplified contours. The object is entirely opaque and lacks any visible seams or joints, suggesting it is carved or cast as a single piece. Its size and mass indicate it is designed to serve as a protective barrier, likely to prevent vehicles from encroaching onto pedestrian areas or to mark boundaries. The color is a consistent, pale white, and there are no visible decorative elements or inscriptions.
description: A solid, pale white guard stone shaped like a simplified human figure with crossed arms and minimal facial features. Its blocky, monolithic form and matte finish suggest durability and a protective function.
description_view_0: Side view showing the profile of the head, nose, and arms crossed over the chest, with the body appearing elongated and upright.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the crossed arms, minimal facial features, and symmetrical, blocky body shape.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the profile of the head and arms, with the same elongated upright stance.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the head and body, with no visible features or details except the general outline.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban sidewalks, near street corners, or at the entrance to driveways to prevent vehicle encroachment and protect pedestrians.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed fully above ground, standing upright and possibly anchored to a solid base for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing at the base and corners due to vehicle contact, as well as general surface erosion and discoloration from weather exposure. The top and sides may accumulate grime or develop a patina over time.
